title: Bolbitis × multipinna
intro: Bolbitis × multipinna is a hybrid species of fern, belonging to the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, where it is valued for its potential to improve cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation, thus exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Bolbitis × multipinna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Bolbitis × multipinna is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for use in traditional remedies, with the most common herbal preparations being teas, tonics, and infusions aimed at promoting cardiovascular well-being.
taxonomy: Bolbitis × multipinna is a hybrid fern species. It belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. Bolbitis × multipinna is a result of a natural cross between two Bolbitis species. The exact parent species of Bolbitis × multipinna are not well-documented. This hybrid is often cultivated for its unique and attractive foliage.
uses: Bolbitis × multipinna has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, but there is limited scientific research on its medicinal properties and potential uses.
